description:
## Background

Beadsmith has no production or legacy users. The current process-working-directory behavior is only an early-development shortcut, so the first Workspace-management slice can replace it outright rather than maintaining a compatibility path, migration layer, or dual RPC contract. The correct first deliverable is a complete user path: start with no Current Workspace, choose a valid Beadwork repository, and use its Issue Explorer data.

This slice deliberately includes the backend foundation because explicit command scope, root validation, durable Workspace state, and the native chooser are one product boundary. Separating them would create temporary compatibility machinery for a user population that does not exist and would make ownership harder to understand.

## What to build

Replace cwd-based Workspace resolution with a complete first-time Current Workspace flow. Beadsmith starts with an empty chooser, lets the user select a directory using the native picker, validates and normalizes it to a Beadwork Git root, loads All Issues, Ready, and Blocked from that root, durably records it as Current, and only then renders the complete Issue Explorer snapshot.

## Technical Approach

- Remove process-cwd Workspace resolution, runtime cwd mutation, the startup `--workspace` override, and the old pathless compatibility RPC rather than supporting them alongside the new model.
- Create one cohesive backend Workspace service. It owns a simple persisted state containing remembered root paths, optional Current Workspace, optional Pending Workspace, request generation, and typed error state. It is the only owner of catalog mutation, validation, loading coordination, and persistence.
- Extend the existing command-runner seam so every Beadwork command receives an explicit working directory. Validate a candidate through Beadwork configuration inspection and independently discover its Git root; never infer identity from configuration output or process cwd. A selected subdirectory normalizes to its root and cannot become a separate Workspace.
- Persist Workspace state in one backend-owned store with explicit save boundaries. There is no existing user data to migrate and no compatibility schema to support. Build proposed state from a stable snapshot, save it, then publish it to the service and UI; a failed save must be rolled back or discarded before a later mutation can run.
- If local Workspace memory is unreadable, show a clear local-storage failure with retry and an explicit reset action. Resetting Workspace memory affects only Beadsmith local state, never a repository or Beadwork data. Do not silently overwrite unreadable memory.
- Register the narrow native-dialog permission needed for one directory picker. Picker cancellation is a no-op. Store access remains backend-only; the frontend receives typed Workspace state and invokes typed select/retry/remove operations but never accesses the store or supplies paths to Issue Explorer loading.
- Show the compact inline selector in the existing sidebar Current Workspace area. With no Current Workspace it presents an empty chooser and Choose folder action. On an invalid candidate it renders inline feedback. On a valid candidate that later fails to load or save, retain it as retryable known state but do not present it as Current.
- Use a fresh supported app-data/store location for Rust, integration, and real-desktop tests. The harness configures where the application resolves storage but never writes a store file directly or touches a developer catalog.

## Considerations

- A true-empty Beadwork repository is a successful Current Workspace and must show the existing true-empty Issue Explorer state, not a failure.
- A valid candidate becomes known after validation; it becomes Current only after all three Issue Explorer views and the final durable save succeed.
- The Workspace Catalog remains capped at 100 paths and uses ordinary validated root paths. Custom names and symlink alias deduplication are out of scope.
- This slice intentionally replaces the old development mechanism in one change. It must leave the app and its test suite green using the new empty-start and typed-selection path, not preserve obsolete launch behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Production code contains no Workspace resolution through `env::current_dir`, runtime cwd mutation, or a `--workspace` launch override.
- [ ] A first launch with no Workspace memory shows an accessible empty chooser rather than loading the app launch directory or rendering a process-cwd error.
- [ ] Choosing a valid initialized Beadwork repository loads All, Ready, and Blocked through explicit cwd and presents the validated Git root as Current only after durable save success.
- [ ] A selected subdirectory resolves to the repository root; non-Git, uninitialized, missing-command, unreadable, and execution failures produce typed inline feedback without changing Current Workspace.
- [ ] Proposed catalog/current state is committed only after explicit store save; failed saves cannot leak partial state into a later operation.
- [ ] Unreadable Workspace memory is recoverable through explicit retry/reset without altering repositories or Beadwork data.
- [ ] The native picker is correctly wired, cancellation changes nothing, and the frontend has neither store access nor a workspace-path argument on Issue Explorer loading.
- [ ] Existing populated and true-empty Issue Explorer desktop scenarios pass after starting empty and using the normal typed selection operation.

## Test Plan

- Rust unit tests for explicit cwd on every Beadwork view command, root normalization, validation failures, proposed-save-commit/rollback, and local-memory reset.
- Typed RPC integration tests proving Workspace state/select/retry/remove operations and path-free Issue Explorer loading.
- Frontend integration tests for empty chooser, picker cancellation, inline validation feedback, successful initial load, retryable initial load failure, and storage-failure reset.
- Real desktop WebDriver tests with a fresh app-data location per scenario: launch empty, select populated and true-empty disposable Workspaces through typed transport, and prove no developer store is consulted.
- Run Rust tests, frontend tests, generated binding checks, `pnpm check`, TypeScript checking, and the migrated desktop suite.

comments.text:
## Product boundary

Done means a first-time user can actually select one valid Workspace and use Beadsmith normally. The selector must not expose a candidate as Current until validation, all three Beadwork views, and store save have succeeded.

Changelog: this slice is now blocked by the two foundation subtasks, not by a monolithic foundation task. It must consume the explicit command scope and transactional state service; it may not recreate cwd, store, root-normalization, or mutation-ordering logic in the UI layer.

title: Select and persist an initial Current Workspace
